description: A designed memorial tribute card ('Wall of Honor' / 'End of Watch'). The background is a dark, somber photo of deep-red roses lying beside a furled American flag on a dark surface. Inset at the upper left is a framed older portrait photograph of the honoree, a mustached man in a dark suit, light shirt, and patterned red-and-white tie, holding a wooden award plaque. Across the lower portion is white serif text reading 'Wall of Honor' (large) beside an ICE/HSI special-agent badge ringed with a blue mourning band; to the right: 'Special Agent Thomas M. Wischerth', then a gold-ruled divider and the phrase 'End of Watch', with the date 'Sunday, March 26, 2017' below. Visual style: reverent, dark palette with roses and flag iconography, elegant serif typography and the agency badge. Messaging/propaganda technique: a fallen-officer memorial that honors agency sacrifice and reinforces ICE's mission/legitimacy ('pledges its continued resolve to ensure public safety and national security'). Target audience: agency personnel, supporters, and the broader public, building goodwill and institutional pride.
notable_text: Wall of Honor; Special Agent Thomas M. Wischerth; End of Watch; Sunday, March 26, 2017
